title: handroanthus ochraceus subsp. neochrysanthus
intro: Handroanthus ochraceus subsp. neochrysanthus, also known as the yellow pochote, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bignoniaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The health benefits of this plant are numerous, including its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In traditional herbal medicine, Handroanthus ochraceus subsp. neochrysanthus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
